Fix redraw ghost when closing About dialog (#72)

* Initial plan

* Fix ghost when closing About dialog via OK button

After show_dialog's event loop exits, post a fresh RefreshStencil
followed by invalidate_window for all visible windows, then flush with
repost_messages().

Root cause: destroy_window posts kWindowMessageRefreshStencil (sentinel
target = (window_t*)1) then queues paint messages for overlapping windows
via invalidate_overlaps.  The recursive clear_window_children call then
destroys each child window; each child's destroy_window re-posts
RefreshStencil, and the dedup logic nullifies the earlier one and
appends a new one *after* the already-queued paint messages.  So
repost_messages() inside the loop processes paint calls against a stale
stencil that still carries the dialog's window ID, and fill_rect for
the background windows is scissored out - leaving a visual ghost.

The X-button path calls end_dialog from dispatch_message at the top
level, so the same ordering issue exists, but dialog children receive
no extra mouse-driven invalidate_window calls that would further perturb
queue ordering.

The fix posts one authoritative RefreshStencil + repaint pass after all
dialog windows are fully torn down, ensuring the stencil is current
before any painting happens.

commit.message:
Fix slice assignment invalidation precision

Same-length slice replacement now stamps only the replaced indices
instead of everything from the first replaced index to the end of the
array.  Different-length replacement (which shifts elements) still
bumps from the first affected index onward.

Also fixes a pre-existing bug where pure insertion via empty slice
(e.g. arr[2:2] = [9]) failed to invalidate shifted proxies.

commit.message:
Fix KeysView.__or__/__xor__ to accept any iterable

Python's dict_keys set operations accept any iterable, not just sets.
Our __or__ and __xor__ delegated to set.__or__/set.__xor__ which
reject non-set operands like lists and tuples.

Fix by converting the other operand to a set first via iterable_to_pyset
helper before calling the set method.

Refactor subscript dispatch with SubscriptKey enum

Replace the mixed key/item dispatch in __getitem__, __setitem__, and
__delitem__ with a unified SubscriptKey enum that resolves proxy keys
and classifies key type (Str/Int/Slice/Other) once before borrowing
the document.

- Add SubscriptKey enum and resolve_subscript_key() in item_ops
- Add subscript_type_error() helper for wrong-key-type errors
- Split item_setitem into item_setitem_str/item_setitem_int
- Split item_delitem into item_delitem_str/item_delitem_int
- item_delitem_str delegates to table_pop (no duplication)
- Move table_pop from item_ops to dict_ops (correct module home)
- Remove dead code: item_getitem, item_setitem, item_delitem,
  require_str_key, require_int_key
- Support proxy keys in Document/DictProxy get/getitem/setdefault
- Add extract_key_str for Document string-only key resolution
- Preserve Python dict error semantics (KeyError for dict misses,
  TypeError for wrong key types)

Refactor exit criteria and add weighted circuit support (#185)

* Refactor exit-criteria to nested object format

Change from flat exit_criteria/threshold fields to nested exit-criteria
object with metric and threshold sub-fields. This modernizes the YAML
schema and prepares for weighted circuit support.

- triforce.yaml: all circuit entries use new exit-criteria format
- scenario_wrapper.py: new ExitCriteria Pydantic model, TrainingCircuitEntry
  uses Field(alias='exit-criteria') for kebab-case YAML keys
- train.py: extract metric/threshold from nested ExitCriteria object

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add kind and weight fields to training circuit models

- TrainingCircuitDefinition gets kind field (sequential or weighted)
- TrainingCircuitEntry gets optional weight field
- Validation: weighted circuits require weight on all entries,
  sequential circuits must not have weight
- Existing circuits default to kind=sequential and continue to work

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add WeightedScenarioSelector and switch_scenario support

- WeightedScenarioSelector: centralized class tracking per-scenario step
  counts. Single update(scenario, steps) -> next_scenario method for RPC.
- ScenarioWrapper: switch_scenario() swaps critic, conditions, room
  selector, metrics. reset() integrates with weighted selector when present.
  Step counter tracks steps per episode for selector reporting.
- StateChangeWrapper: switch_scenario() updates objective, per_reset/room/frame.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add weighted env factory and scenario-aware MetricTracker

- zelda_env.py: make_weighted_zelda_env() creates env stack with
  ScenarioWrapper connected to a WeightedScenarioSelector
- ml_ppo_worker.py: WeightedEnvFactory carries scenario defs, action
  space, and selector proxy for worker subprocesses
- metrics.py: MetricTracker now buffers per-scenario metrics during
  weighted mode scenario switches. get_metrics_and_clear() returns
  {scenario: {metric: value}} in weighted mode, flat dict normally.
- ScenarioWrapper passes scenario_name to MetricTracker in weighted mode

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add weighted training loop to PPO

- train_weighted(): public entry point for weighted circuit training
- _train_weighted_single(): single-env mode, uses selector directly
- _train_weighted_multi(): multi-env mode, exposes WeightedScenarioSelector

- _check_weighted_exit_criteria(): per-scenario exit criteria checking

Preserve array boundary spacing on insert/append/extend

When appending, extending, or inserting at the end of an array, the
old last element's suffix (space before `]`) was left in place,
producing corrupted output like `[1, 2 , 3]`. Fix by stripping the
suffix before mutation and reapplying it to the new last element.

When inserting at index 0, the old first element's prefix (space
after `[`) was lost. Fix by copying it to the new first element;
the old prefix naturally becomes inter-element spacing.

Extract value_prefix/value_suffix helpers to deduplicate the
repeated decor-reading pattern across boundary helpers and
save_removal_decor.

Fix Document.__delitem__/pop double-borrow panic with proxy keys

Both methods used &mut self, causing PyO3 to hold a mutable borrow
while key.extract() ran Python code on proxy keys — triggering a
double-borrow panic. Switch to slf: &Bound<Self> and use
extract_key_str (which safely resolves proxies before any borrow).

Also switch DictProxy.pop to use extract_key_str for consistency.

commit.message:
Replace shift tracking with per-element stamping in MutationTrie

Remove the shifted_from/shifts mechanism from TrieNode entirely. Array
mutations now stamp each affected index individually via stamp_child,
matching the O(n) cost toml_edit already pays to shift elements.

This eliminates two false-staleness bugs:
- stamp_shift min-compression lost precision when two shifts had
  increasing thresholds (min collapsed them)
- stamp_child did not clear shifted_from, so old shift metadata
  poisoned later shifts after an array was replaced

The trie now has just two operations: stamp and stamp_child.
Affected::Shift(usize) becomes Affected::Range { from, to }.

commit.message:
feat: build-and-push.sh for local dev + conditional pull secret in bootstrap.sh (#38)

* fix: detect private GHCR recipe packages and inject OCI credentials into Radius

The Radius operator (applications-rp in radius-system) uses the ORAS library
to pull OCI Bicep recipe artifacts. It has no Docker login, so private GHCR
packages cause a 401 at deploy time even though the bootstrap user can pull them.

Fixes:
- Add check_recipe_anonymous_access(): tests access without credentials using a
  temp empty Docker config — mirrors exactly what the Radius operator sees
- Add ensure_radius_oci_credentials(): called after recipe publishing, before
  env deploy. If packages are private and GHCR_TOKEN/GHCR_USERNAME are set,
  creates a radius-oci-recipe-creds secret in radius-system and configures
  applications-rp via kubectl set volume + kubectl set env (both idempotent)
  to mount the credentials and set DOCKER_CONFIG=/oci-creds so ORAS picks them up.
  If credentials are not set, fails early with actionable instructions.
- Also fixes jq parse errors in fetch_env_namespace/fetch_env_id (sed strip)
- prepare-cluster.sh next-step now propagates --create-spn when CREATE_SPN=true
- Docs (README, walkthrough, checklist) updated to include --create-spn examples

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use Bicep safe access operator and add GHCR pre-flight warning

- app.bicep: replace contains()+ternary with .?parameter ?? default on
  stateStoreRecipeParams, pubsubRecipeParams, secretStoreRecipeParams
  to silence use-safe-access linter warnings
- bootstrap.sh: warn at pre-flight if GHCR_TOKEN/GHCR_USERNAME are
  unset and RECIPE_REGISTRY is on ghcr.io, before any long-running
  recipe publish/deploy steps

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: inject GHCR creds into Radius via strategic merge patch + auto-populate from gh CLI

kubectl set volume --add is not available on all kubectl versions.
Replace with strategic merge patch (for volumes) + JSON patch (for volumeMount
idempotency check + add). The env var (DOCKER_CONFIG) still uses kubectl set env
which is stable across versions.

Also: auto-populate GHCR_USERNAME and GHCR_TOKEN from the gh CLI at pre-flight
when not explicitly set, so bootstrap doesn't require manual export if the user
is already authenticated with 'gh auth login'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(bootstrap): replace cluster-patching OCI auth with public-access gate

Remove ensure_radius_oci_credentials() which patched applications-rp at
runtime (unsustainable — overwritten on every rad install/upgrade).

Replace with require_public_recipe_access() which checks anonymous OCI
access for each recipe artifact before deploying. If any package is
private, the script fails fast with direct GitHub web UI URLs to change
visibility.

Recipe artifacts (state-store, pubsub, secrets) contain only Bicep
templates — no secrets — so making them public is correct for this demo
project. See docs/adr/ghcr-recipe-packages-public.md for full rationale.

Also update the GHCR pre-flight comment to reflect the real reasons
GHCR_TOKEN is needed (publish + app image pull secret, not recipe auth).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add ADR-0002 — GHCR recipe packages must be public

Documents the decision to make GHCR OCI recipe artifacts publicly
accessible, with full rationale covering the four rejected alternatives
(K8s secret injection, GitHub REST API, rad credential, rad env update).

Includes a bootstrap gate (require_public_recipe_access) that enforces
this and prints direct GitHub web UI URLs if any package is private.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(recipes): rename tenantId→azureTenantId in secrets recipe

Parameter was named 'tenantId' in secrets.bicep while state-store and
pubsub used 'azureTenantId'. The environment Bicep passes 'azureTenantId'
to all three recipes, causing an InvalidTemplate error at deploy time.

Aligns the parameter name across all three recipes.
Recipes must be republished for the fix to take effect.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(bootstrap): auto-detect arm64→amd64 platform mismatch for container builds

When the build host is Apple Silicon (arm64) but the cluster nodes are
amd64 (standard AKS), docker build silently produces an arm64 image that
fails with 'no match for platform in manifest' at deploy time.

Auto-detect the mismatch by comparing uname -m with the cluster node
architecture and default IMAGE_PLATFORM to linux/amd64 in that case.
--image-platform flag still overrides this auto-detection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(bootstrap): add --setup-workload-identity flag

Runs 'az aks update --enable-oidc-issuer --enable-workload-identity'
before credential registration so workload identity mode works in a
single bootstrap invocation. Forces --azure-auth-mode wi when triggered
in auto mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(bootstrap): auto-discover AKS cluster name when default doesn't exist

The default AKS_CLUSTER_NAME is 'radiusclaim-aks' but actual clusters
may use a different naming convention (e.g. wesback-aks-radiusclaim).
When --setup-workload-identity is used and the configured name is not
found, auto-discover the first cluster in the resource group via
'az aks list' and continue. Fails fast with a clear message if the
resource group has no clusters at all.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update bootstrap flag examples for --setup-workload-identity and arm64 platform

- Add --setup-workload-identity flag to initial bootstrap examples (lines 37-41)
- Document two-step workload identity flow (first-time cluster setup vs. subsequent deploys)
- Add macOS note about arm64→amd64 auto-detection and --image-platform flag
- Update Step 2 bootstrap example to include --setup-workload-identity for clarity

These changes reflect recent bootstrap.sh enhancements that auto-detect platform mismatches on Apple Silicon and simplify first-time AKS cluster setup with federated credentials.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: patch default SA with ghcr-pull-secret after rad deploy

Radius silently drops the runtimes.kubernetes.pod.spec.imagePullSecrets
override, leaving all 3 pods in ImagePullBackOff after rad app deploy.

Add patch_pull_secret_to_serviceaccount() to bootstrap.sh. It runs after
rad deploy, before wait_for_deployment. Steps:
- Re-creates ghcr-pull-secret if Radius cleared it during namespace lifecycle
- Patches the default SA with imagePullSecrets idempotently (merge-patch)
- Deletes any ImagePullBackOff pods so they restart immediately

Skipped silently when GHCR_TOKEN/GHCR_USERNAME are unset. Dry-run safe.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Root cause: service image packages are private on GHCR despite
public repo. Created issues #33-#36 covering package visibility,

Decision: make packages public — simplest path for a reference sample.

Add scripts/build-and-push.sh for local dev image build+push workflow.
Supports --registry, --tag, --platform flags; defaults to GHCR with

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(scripts): fix bash assignment bug in bootstrap.sh causing =radiusclaim-aks error

Line 1270 (a comment) reported '=radiusclaim-aks: command not found' because the
argument parser handled --cluster-name VALUE (two-token) but not --cluster-name=VALUE
(equals-sign, one-token) form. Passing --cluster-name=radiusclaim-aks fell through
to the *) catch-all, and the error message at that point incorrectly told users to
pass --aks-cluster-name (which the parser also didn't know), compounding the confusion.

Root cause: missing --cluster-name=*) and --aks-cluster-name[=*]) cases, plus a
wrong flag name in the auto-discovery fail message.

Changes:
- Add --cluster-name=*) case: strips prefix, captures value, shifts by 1
- Add --aks-cluster-name) case: alias matching prepare-cluster.sh flag name
- Add --aks-cluster-name=*) case: same alias, equals-sign form
- Fix fail message: 'Pass --aks-cluster-name' -> 'Pass --cluster-name <name>'

Now --cluster-name radiusclaim-aks, --cluster-name=radiusclaim-aks,
--aks-cluster-name radiusclaim-aks, and --aks-cluster-name=radiusclaim-aks
all work identically and idempotently.

Fixes reported by Wesley Backelant

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(dustmote): add ambient floating dust particle system for atmospheric realism

Adds a new dust mote system that renders floating atmospheric particles
visible in light beams. This addresses the 'artificial environments'
visual realism problem by adding lived-in atmospheric detail.

Key features:
- Brownian motion simulation for realistic particle drift
- Light-reactive visibility (particles only visible when illuminated)
- Genre-specific particle types (dust, spores, ash, digital glitch, pollen)
- Soft-edged rendering with glow effects for spores/digital particles
- Light tinting from nearby torch/muzzle flash sources
- Zero-allocation update loop (4.5µs/op benchmark)

Genre presets:
- Fantasy: warm dust motes catching torchlight
- Sci-fi: minimal clean environment particles
- Horror: sickly greenish spores with subtle glow
- Cyberpunk: neon digital glitch particles with trails
- Post-apocalyptic: heavy ash/fallout density

Integration:
- System initialized in NewGame() with dustmote.NewSystem()
- SetGenre() called in trySetGenre() cascade
- Update in updateV3Systems() with camera position and light collection
- Render in renderCombatEffects() after weather particles
- Lights collected from torches (with flicker) and muzzle flashes

Test coverage: 55% (exceeds 40% target)
Benchmark: 4,485 ns/op, 0 B/op, 0 allocs/op

Precise array shift invalidation for list mutations

Add shifted_from field to MutationTrie for range-based proxy
invalidation.  Mid-array insert, remove, pop, and slice operations now
only invalidate proxies at or after the shift point — earlier proxies
survive.

  Option<Key> return type from list mutation helpers
- Add Affected::for_removal() constructor to deduplicate the
  end-of-array optimisation across Array and AoT arms
- item_insert returns Option<Affected> for consistency
- stamp_shift on MutationTrie accumulates by taking min(threshold)
- stamp() clears shifted_from since revised_at subsumes it
- Slice deletion uses bump_shift(min_idx); slice assignment uses
  bump_shift(start)
- 7 new trie unit tests, 8 new Python staleness tests
